summary: Full backend + frontend testing complete for Vishnu Raghav Platform. 29/29 pytest backend tests passed. All critical frontend flows verified: hero, courses, books, blog (search+category), newsletter, contact, registration, login (student+admin), mock-payment enrollment, course player with YouTube iframe, dashboard with all 5 tabs and enrolled course listing, and locked screen for non-enrolled users.

minor.issue: CORSMiddleware uses allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False. This works for current header-based JWT auth (Authorization: Bearer) but the login/register endpoints also set httpOnly cookies which will NOT be sent cross-origin by browsers with this CORS config. Cookie path is fine since frontend stores token in localStorage and uses it via Bearer header — so functional behavior is unaffected, but the cookie set on login is effectively unused for cross-origin requests.

action_items: Optional: If author later wants email-magic-link login or any flow relying on httpOnly cookie auth cross-origin, update CORS to explicit allowed origin + allow_credentials=True. For current Bearer-token usage, no action needed.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~630 lines — manageable but borderline. As more admin endpoints (e.g., admin CRUD for books/blog) get added, split into routers/auth.py, routers/enrollment.py, routers/admin.py.
Static data (BOOKS, COURSES, BLOG_POSTS) lives in server.py. Move to /app/backend/data/*.py modules for cleaner separation when content grows.
JWT_SECRET is hardcoded as fallback only via env (good) and ADMIN_PASSWORD env is read at startup — seed_admin correctly updates password if changed (verified).

Razorpay live path in /api/enrollments/checkout is partially implemented (creates order_id locally with secrets.token_hex instead of calling Razorpay Orders API). When user provides live keys this needs to be replaced with actual razorpay-python client call. This is acceptable for now because mock mode is the documented active mode.
PUT/PATCH /api/auth/profile uses model_dump(exclude_unset=True) correctly. Good practice.
All MongoDB _id fields are stringified in serializers — no _id leaks in any tested endpoint response.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@vishnuraghav.in / Admin@12345. Student: registered fresh TEST_student_<ts>@example.com and ui_student_<ts>@example.com via UI during tests.
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_-prefixed users, one newsletter subscription, one contact entry, and a few mock-paid enrollments for time-management-mastery during testing. Safe to clean later via Mongo if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_vishnu_raghav.py covers all 29 endpoint scenarios (health, books, courses, blog, auth, profile, enrollment+mock payment+already-enrolled+progress, newsletter, contact, admin). Frontend testids are all wired correctly: hero-cta-courses/books, enroll-<slug>, book-amazon-<slug>, blog-search, blog-cat-<category>, nav-getstarted, nav-signin, nav-dashboard, auth-tab-register/login, auth-name/email/password/submit, pay-modal/confirm/success/success-watch, course-player, locked-screen, dashboard-page, dash-tab-courses/progress/certs/payments/profile, dash-course-<slug>, nl-email/submit, contact-name/email/message/submit.
